fields.name: Analysis Area
fields.tooltip:
Contains the application area of the analysis line template. The following options are available:
Sales: The analysis line template was set up in Sales & Receivables
Purchase: The analysis line template was set up in Purchases & Payables
Inventory: The analysis line template was set up in Inventory

fields.name: Analysis Line Template Name
fields.tooltip:
Contains the name of the analysis line template.
The program automatically retrieves the name from the Name field in the Analysis Line Template table.

fields.name: Line No.
fields.tooltip:
Contains the number of the analysis line.
The program fills in this field automatically and uses the number to keep track of the various lines in the analysis report.

fields.name: Row Ref. No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a row reference number for the analysis line.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies a description for the analysis line.

fields.name: Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of totaling for the analysis line. The type determines which items within the totaling range that you specify in the Range field will be totaled.

fields.name: Range
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number or formula of the type to use to calculate the total for this line.

fields.name: New Page
fields.tooltip: Specifies if you want a page break after the current line when you print the analysis report.

fields.name: Show
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether you want the analysis line to be included when you print the report.

fields.name: Bold
fields.tooltip: Specifies if you want the amounts on this line to be printed in bold.

fields.name: Italic
fields.tooltip: Specifies if you want the amounts in this line to be printed in italics.

fields.name: Underline
fields.tooltip: Specifies if you want the amounts in this line to be underlined when printed.

fields.name: Show Opposite Sign
fields.tooltip: Specifies if you want sales and negative adjustments to be shown as positive amounts and purchases and positive adjustments to be shown as negative amounts.

fields.name: Source Type Filter
fields.tooltip:
Contains the source type filter that you want to filter your analysis report by.
Available source types are Item, Customer and Vendor. When you have selected the source type, you must also enter the filter in the Source No. Filter field.

fields.name: Date Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the date interval that values are filtered by.
If this field contains starting and ending dates for a date interval, then other field values only show information for that interval.
For more information, see How to: Enter Dates and Times.

fields.name: Item Budget Filter
fields.tooltip: Contains a code for the budget that you want to link this analysis line to. The budget code determines whether items from specific budgets will be included in the printed report.

fields.name: Location Filter
fields.tooltip: Contains a code for the location that you want to link this analysis line to. The location code determines whether items from specific locations will be included in the printed report.

fields.name: Dimension 1 Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the filter to use on item ledger entries or item analysis view entries in an analysis report.
If the Item Analysis View Code field in the Analysis Line Template window is blank, you can enter a Global Dimension 1 value code here that the program automatically links the analysis line to.
If you have entered a code in the Item Analysis View Code field in the Analysis Line Template window, you can select a value code from those available for Dimension 1 in the selected item analysis view. The analysis line will then be linked to that dimension value.
When you specify the dimension value code, you determine whether accounts for specific dimension values are included in the printed report and in the Analysis Report window.
Click here to learn more about dimensions.

fields.name: Dimension 2 Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the filter to use on item ledger entries or item analysis view entries in an analysis report.
If the Item Analysis View Name field in the Analysis Line Template window blank, you can enter a Global Dimension 1 value code here that the program automatically links the analysis line to.
If you have entered a code in the Item Analysis View Code field in the Analysis Line Template window, you can select a value code from those available for Dimension 2 in the selected item analysis view. The analysis line will then be linked to that dimension value.
When you specify the dimension value code, you determine whether accounts for specific dimension values are included in the printed report and in the Analysis Report window.
Click here to learn more about dimensions.

fields.name: Dimension 3 Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the filter to use on item ledger entries or item analysis view entries in an analysis report.
If the Item Analysis View Name field in the Analysis Line Template window blank, you can enter a Global Dimension 1 value code here that the program automatically links the analysis line to.
If you have entered a code in the Item Analysis View Code field in the Analysis Line Template window, you can select a value code from those available for Dimension 3 in the selected item analysis view. The analysis line will then be linked to that dimension value.
When you specify the dimension value code, you determine whether accounts for specific dimension values are included in the printed report and in the Analysis Report window.
Click here to learn more about dimensions.

fields.name: Dimension 1 Totaling
fields.tooltip: Specifies which dimension value amounts will be totaled on this line.

fields.name: Dimension 2 Totaling
fields.tooltip: Specifies which dimension value amounts will be totaled on this line. If the type on the line is Formula, this field must be blank. Also, if you do not want the amounts on the line to be filtered by dimensions, this field must be blank.

fields.name: Dimension 3 Totaling
fields.tooltip: Specifies which dimension value amounts will be totaled on this line. If the type on the line is Formula, this field must be blank. Also, if you do not want the amounts on the line to be filtered by dimensions, this field must be blank.

fields.name: Source No. Filter
fields.tooltip:
Contains the number of the source type you want to use as a filter in your analysis report.
Depending on what you select in the Source Type Filter field (customer, item or vendor), you can enter a filter in this field to filter on the customer, vendor or item numbers.

fields.name: Group Dimension Code
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Indentation
fields.tooltip: Specifies the indentation of the line.
